title: neogaillonia incana
intro: Neogaillonia incana, commonly known as Gailonia, is a plant species that belongs to the family Annonaceae. It is used in traditional medicine to provide health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. The therapeutic actions of Neogaillonia incana include its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in the treatment of various health conditions. The bioactive constituents of Neogaillonia incana include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
side_effects: Neogaillonia incana can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and gastrointestinal problems. Ingesting large quantities of the plant can lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, as well as interactions with certain medications. Prolonged exposure to the plant's sap or oils can cause skin rashes, itching, and inflammation, while excessive consumption may also result in headaches and dizziness. Improper use of Neogaillonia incana can also exacerbate underlying health conditions, such as kidney or liver disease.
